en.extract:
Everett's thrush (Zoothera everetti) is a species of bird in the family Turdidae.  The name commemorates British colonial administrator and zoological collector Alfred Hart Everett.


Distribution and habitat
The thrush is endemic to the island of Borneo where it has been recorded only from the mountains of Sabah and northern Sarawak in eastern Malaysia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.  It is threatened by habitat loss.

'''Everett's thrush''' ('''''Zoothera everetti''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Turdidae]].  The name commemorates British colonial administrator and zoological collector [[Alfred Hart Everett]].

==Distribution and habitat==
The thrush is [[endemic]] to the island of [[Borneo]] where it has been recorded only from the mountains of [[Sabah]] and northern [[Sarawak]] in eastern [[Malaysia]].<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=  Phillipps, Karen |name-list-style= amp |year=2011 |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 }}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.  It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
